The '''Corragut''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] [[Homeworld/Legends|hailing]] from [[Altawar]], a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds']] [[Corellian sector/Legends|Corellian sector]]. After the species was discovered by [[Corellian/Legends|Corellians]], the Corragut were offered opportunities upon introduction to the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. However, they subsequently became a "client planet" for the planet [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]]—which was governed by a [[Human/Legends|Human]]-centric culture—and had little say in governing themselves.

==Society and culture==
The Corragut were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Altawar]], in the [[Corellian sector/Legends|Corellian sector]] of the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]]. The species was capable of making crafts and goods from Altawar's resources and aspired to trade these with other locations in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] upon their introduction to [[Galaxy/Legends|galactic]] affairs.<ref name="SOF" />

==History==
For some time, the species maintained a general ignorance of the galaxy's ongoing events, and their technology was considered "backward" by the standards of some [[Human/Legends|Human]] cultures. Early in the [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the planet [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia's]] expansion, Altawar was discovered by the Corellian [[Sector/Legends|sector]], and therefore the wider galaxy,<ref name="SOF" /> alongside several other worlds and their species<ref name="CCW">''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]''</ref>—such as the [[Frozian/Legends|Frozians]] of the planet [[Froz]] and the [[Nosaurian/Legends|Nosaurians]] of the planet [[New Plympto/Legends|New Plympto]]. The Corragut were excited to interact with other civilizations due to the opportunities presented to them.<ref name="SOF" />

[[File:Corellia_SOF.png|thumb|right|300px|Bureaucrats and policy makers on Corellia (pictured) controlled most of the Corragut's affairs following their introduction to the wider galaxy.]]
However, due to the Corragut's lack of expertise in galactic affairs and general lack of technology compared to Corellia and its Human-centric culture, [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Bureaucrat/Legends|bureaucrats]] and policy makers ultimately rendered Altawar a "client planet" for Corellia, which restricted the species' ability to make trade and defense decisions. The Corellians insisted that their lack of influence was for their safety and prosperity, thus allowing the species to provide tradeable goods to the galaxy while receiving technology in return. However, over an extended period of [[Time/Legends|time]], Corellians restricted the technology given to the Corragut and deprived them of military assets.<ref name="SOF" />

By the time of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]], most Corragut were displeased with the Corellians' overseer-like treatment of their species. However, efforts to change their predicament stalled following the [[Devastation of Froz|devastation]] of the Frozians' homeworld during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] between the Empire and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]], an event that overshadowed the Corragut's situation.<ref name="SOF" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Corragut were first mentioned in the [[2003]] [[roleplaying game]] sourcebook ''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]'', published by [[Wizards of the Coast]] for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="CCW" />
